results.possibleIdentity: Leonard Schaier is an electronic engineer who was active during the late 1960s and 1970s, specializing in sonar, circuitry, and systems. He served as a technical member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to facilitate collaborations between artists and engineers. During the late 1960s, Schaier was employed by the EDO Corporation (referenced in some archival materials as EDD Corporation), specifically within its Government Systems Division based in College Point, New York. His historical records, including E.A.T. index cards, associate him with Mohegan Lake, New York, though he also resided in Brooklyn earlier in the 1960s. An amateur radio enthusiast, he held the call sign W2IVY. In his later years, Schaier became a prominent community activist in Port Washington, Long Island, where he founded the group "Citizens for Quiet Skies over North Hempstead" to advocate against aircraft noise from LaGuardia Airport. He is also an inventor with multiple patents assigned to Vestas Wind Systems related to wind turbine technology and reactive power control, filed in the late 2000s.
